subject: Public Health
title: The War on Drugs: A Public Health Failure
description: A quick win: understand The War on Drugs: A Public Health Failure.

hook.text: Fifty years and $1 trillion later, the War on Drugs has filled prisons but failed to reduce drug use or supply.

buildup.text: Criminalizing drug use pushes people underground—away from treatment, into unsafe practices, and into the justice.

discovery.text: Portugal decriminalized all personal drug use in 2001—drug deaths dropped, HIV infections fell, and treatment increased.

twist.text: Incarceration for drug offenses disproportionately affects minorities—despite similar usage rates across racial groups.

climax.text: A public health approach—treating addiction as a disease rather than a crime—produces better outcomes at lower cost.

punchline.text: You can't arrest your way out of a health crisis. Treatment works.

quiz.question: What happened after Portugal decriminalized personal drug use?
options: Drug deaths dropped and treatment increased
